entries.content: The Internet is a global network of interconnected computers using standardized protocols (TCP/IP) to exchange information. From ARPANET origins to the World Wide Web, it revolutionized communication, commerce, and knowledge sharing. Key technologies include packet switching, DNS, and encryption. The Internet enables unprecedented global connectivity while raising concerns about privacy, security, and digital divides.

entries.content: Blockchain is a distributed ledger technology ensuring transparent, tamper-resistant record-keeping without central authority. Each block contains cryptographically linked transactions, creating an immutable chain. Beyond cryptocurrencies, applications include smart contracts, supply chain tracking, and decentralized finance. Blockchain represents a paradigm shift in trust and coordination mechanisms.
